brief_justification: Paper A is a computational-geometry/topology pipeline: it builds Delaunay tessellations of a cosmic point set and uses domain decomposition (overlapping blocks, circumsphere-filtered tetrahedra, boundary stitching) to extract the Morse-topological filament network at scale. Paper B is a survey of agent-based social simulation, where autonomous agents evolve under behavioral rules (or LLM/data-driven representations). The driving machinery — a global tessellation/topology-extraction algorithm versus rule-based multi-agent dynamics — is entirely different, with no role-for-role correspondence beyond the generic notion of 'computationally modeling a system'.
